name: Safeway — Alabama Ave SE
notes: Double Up Food Bucks at produce section. WIC-approved full-service. Large Latin foods aisle with masa harina, dried chiles, crema. Bus routes 90, 92 stop outside.

name: Giant Food — Alabama Ave SE
notes: Full WIC-authorized store. Good prices on bulk rice, dried beans. Pharmacy on site.

name: Good Food Markets — Benning Rd
notes: Corner store upgraded through Double Up program. Fresh produce added 2023. SNAP-accepted corner store model. Double Up max $10/visit.

name: Mi Tierra Bodega
notes: Latin-owned bodega. Excellent selection of Salvadoran and Central American staples: loroco, masa, queso duro, pupusa ingredients. Walking distance from Rosa's address.

name: Eastern Market Farmers Market
notes: Accepts SNAP EBT via market token system. Double Up up to $20. WIC Farmers Market Nutrition Program (FMNP) and Senior FMNP vouchers accepted. Multiple vendors with Asian produce: bok choy, ginger, scallions.

name: Capital Area Food Bank — Mobile Market
notes: Free food distribution. No income verification at point of service. Serves Ward 7 and Ward 8. Stops near Alabama Ave, Benning Rd, and Congress Heights Metro.

name: DC Central Kitchen — Healthy Corners
notes: Healthy Corners partner store. Fresh produce, healthy snacks at subsidized prices. SNAP accepted. Nutrition education available on-site.
